turns.name: GRISWOLD
turns.text: Mr, Chief Justice, may it please the Court: This is a Federal tax case here on a writ of Tenth Circuit. Although it is a tax case, it is a sequel or of Oklahoma, where this court held the Corporation Commission of Oklahoma had no power to fix minimum rates for the sale of gas. The respondent taxpayer here produces and sells a natural gas. During the years 1952 through 1957 it charged its customers those increased rates pursuant to the order of the Oklahoma Corporation Commission. And, naturally, the amounts which it received were income to it. They entered into the computation of the ''gross income from the property," against which it took in each of those years, '52 to 857, a deduction of 21-1/2 percent as percentage depletion, pursuant to Section 613 of the Internal Revenue Code. There is no doubt that it was entitled to this deduction for depletion,, as it clearly received the gross income under a claim of right and the statute provided that where income is received from oil and gas there is a depletion deduction. The taxpayer has his option to take either cost depletion or percentage depletion. In this case percentage depletion was advantageous, and that was what was taken. the Respondent was sued by several of its customers on the two of them and paid to them an aggregate of $505,536 in 1958. which is before the Court. The question is the consequence The taxpayer deducted the repayment in full. 27-1/2 percent. MR. CHIEF JUSTICE WARREKs We will recess now.
